description_long: This stereo sound system consists of two tall, rectangular speaker towers with a dark wood grain finish on the front and sides. The front face of each tower features two prominent hexagonal speaker cones and a circular bass port at the bottom, all set within a black panel. The sides and rear of the speakers are finished in a solid black color, contrasting with the wood-textured front. Between the two speakers, a vertical control panel is visible, containing several small buttons and possibly a dial, suggesting manual control options. The overall design is modern and angular, with slightly slanted tops and a sturdy, boxy build. This type of sound system would typically be found in urban environments such as home entertainment setups, garages, or community centers.
description: A pair of tall, dark wood-finished stereo speakers with black front panels featuring two speaker cones, a bass port, and a visible control panel between them.
description_view_0: Side view showing the wood grain texture and the angled tops of the two speaker towers.
description_view_1: Rear view displaying the solid black backs of the speakers, with no visible controls or ports.
description_view_2: Opposite side view highlighting the wood grain finish and the angular shape of the speakers.
description_view_3: Front view revealing the two hexagonal speaker cones, circular bass ports, and a central control panel with buttons.
attribute_speaker_grille_pattern: The speaker grilles have a geometric hexagonal pattern around the speaker cones, with a solid black panel and no visible mesh or perforation.
attribute_control_panel_layout: A vertical control panel is located between the two speakers, featuring several small round buttons and possibly a dial, arranged in a single column.
